Notes.Text:
This update for python3-lxml fixes the following issue

- CVE-2026-41066: Information disclosure via untrusted XML input leading to local file read (bsc#1263254).
Notes.Title: Details

Notes.Text: The CVRF data is provided by SUSE under the Creative Commons License 4.0 with Attribution (CC-BY-4.0).
Notes.Title: Terms of Use

Relationships.RelatesToProductReference: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server LTSS Extended Security 12 SP5

Vulnerabilities.Description: lxml is a library for processing XML and HTML in the Python language. Prior to 6.1.0, using either of the two parsers in the default configuration (with resolve_entities=True) allows untrusted XML input to read local files. Setting the resolve_entities option explicitly to resolve_entities='internal' or resolve_entities=False disables the local file access. This vulnerability is fixed in 6.1.0.

ProductID: SUSE Linux Enterprise Server LTSS Extended Security 12 SP5:python3-lxml-3.6.1-3.9.1
